tests.name: Simple bug with clear error
tests.prompt: User says: 'Getting 500 error on /api/users. Error log says Column not found: updated_at'
tests.expected_behavior: Reads the route handler and model. Finds the missing column or missing timestamp config.
assertions: Identifies root cause (timestamps config or missing column)
Proposes fix with specific file and line

tests.name: Authentication bug
tests.prompt: User says: 'Login works but after 1 hour users get a blank screen. They have to login again.'
tests.expected_behavior: Reads auth-related files. Diagnoses token/session expiry issue.
assertions: Reads auth middleware, session config, token refresh logic
Identifies session/token timeout as root cause
Fix is specific (change config value or add refresh logic)
Mentions verification: login, wait for expiry, confirm session persists

tests.name: Complex bug escalates to IMPROVE
tests.prompt: User says: 'Everything is slow. Pages take 10+ seconds. All of them.'
tests.expected_behavior: This is not a single bug — it's a systemic issue. Recommend switching to IMPROVE mode.
assertions: Recognizes this is not a single bug
Recommends IMPROVE mode for systematic analysis
Does NOT attempt to fix everything in FIX mode
May do a quick check (database indexes, N+1) but flags the scope

tests.name: Fix with side effects warning
tests.prompt: User says: 'The delete button on projects doesn't work. Nothing happens when I click it.'
tests.expected_behavior: Reads the delete handler and frontend component. Fixes the issue but warns about cascade effects.
assertions: Reads both backend route and frontend component
Identifies if it's a frontend issue (event handler) or backend (route/controller)
If fix involves cascade deletes, warns about related data
Verification includes checking that related data is handled
